title: Ci release 1.19
body:
There's no sense in wasting resources on extensive testing for branches
which see very little if any changes.  Trim down automation to the
minimum viable jobs.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/release-1.19/OWNERS)~~ [cevich] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Okay I think this is ready. The diff is a bit hard to read (I re-arranged the tasks), but [effectively these are the only tasks that will run](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3353/files#diff-62587956f943bb2503db7bc6dd27d0d888074a1c0ecaab3f570ad611aff0f7bbR142-R145). So unit and integration are gone, the only thing we check is "Does it compile" and "Does it pass docker conformance".
